observation: All endpoints average 3.1–3.8s response time — 6–8x above typical 500ms production target for public API
root_cause_hypothesis: PHP-FPM cold path + SQLite/development DB I/O on each request; no opcode-cache warm-up in dev env; local Windows dev machine not Linux production server
recommendation: 1) Enable PHP OPcache; 2) Use PostgreSQL connection pooling; 3) Profile with Xdebug/Blackfire to isolate top 3 slow queries; 4) Add HTTP response caching (Redis) for read-heavy API endpoints; 5) Re-test on Linux staging server to separate dev-env overhead

observation: Integration middleware rejection latency 3237ms avg — 62% above 2000ms threshold; SCENARIO FAIL
root_cause_hypothesis: Middleware chain executes full PHP bootstrap even for rejected requests; no short-circuit before database/service layer; possible eager service provider loading on every request
recommendation: 1) Move auth token validation to earliest-possible middleware with short-circuit return; 2) Avoid loading CatalogReadService/DB connections before auth check; 3) Add integration API route-level middleware cache; 4) Consider Nginx-level JWT pre-validation

observation: Web catalog page averages 3784ms with 130KB HTML payload — largest response size and slowest API endpoint
root_cause_hypothesis: Full server-side rendered Blade template includes embedded catalog data in a single HTML response; no fragment caching; CDN not configured
recommendation: 1) Enable Blade partial caching via Laravel cache tags; 2) Split catalog data fetch from HTML rendering; 3) Compress HTML responses with Gzip/Brotli; 4) Paginate catalog results on server; 5) CDN for static assets

observation: Throughput 0.26–0.33 rps across all scenarios — severely limiting concurrent user capacity
root_cause_hypothesis: Sequential request model (1 VU) with 3–4s avg latency yields natural ~0.29 rps ceiling; actual production concurrency would be much higher
recommendation: 1) Full load test with k6 or Locust at 50–100 concurrent VUs to determine actual capacity ceiling; 2) PHP-FPM pool size tuning; 3) Async queue for heavy operations; 4) Database read replicas for catalog queries

observation: /api/v1/subjects p95=3969ms — highest p95 variance of all API endpoints
root_cause_hypothesis: Subjects endpoint may perform a GROUP BY or aggregate query without proper index; first-request overhead from cold query plan cache; variable row count per request
recommendation: 1) Add composite index on subjects table; 2) Cache subjects list in Redis with 5-minute TTL; 3) Review query plan via EXPLAIN ANALYZE

observation: /news endpoint returns HTTP 500 under test — cannot measure news module load performance
evidence: HTTP 500 confirmed from phase2 and phase3 pre-test check
root_cause_hypothesis: Underlying error in news controller/view — likely missing DB record or template variable binding failure
recommendation: 1) Fix /news controller error; 2) Add integration test asserting /news returns 200; 3) Check LibraryNews model seeder and DB migration

observation: /api/login endpoint times out (>10s) — cannot measure authentication flow under load
evidence: Timeout confirmed from phase2; /api/login not included in phase3 scenarios
root_cause_hypothesis: Laravel Sanctum token generation may be triggering slow hashing or DB query; possible misconfiguration of session/cookie driver in dev env
recommendation: 1) Profile /api/login with Xdebug; 2) Verify bcrypt cost factor is ≤12 in dev; 3) Check Sanctum token cleanup queries

observation: Endurance test (40 req/138s) shows consistent latency — no memory leak detected but no warm-up improvement
root_cause_hypothesis: PHP-FPM workers are long-lived enough to reuse opcode cache but no per-process query caching; stable behavior confirms no regression but also no warm-up benefit
recommendation: 1) Implement in-memory result caching (Redis); 2) Pre-warm PHP-FPM workers before load tests; 3) Endurance tests over longer window (>10 min) on staging
